---
title: "Bio"
---

![](profile.png){fig-align="center" width="230"}

### Anuththara Rupasinghe, Ph.D. {.bio-name}

<p class="bio-position">Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Princeton University</p>

**Machine Learning | Statistical Signal Processing | Data Science | Computational Neuroscience | Biomedical AI**

I am a machine learning and a computational neuroscience researcher with a **Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering** and postdoctoral training at the Princeton Neuroscience Institute. My work focuses on developing statistical and machine learning methods for analyzing complex neural and biological data, with an emphasis on probabilistic modeling, statistical signal processing, time-series analysis, uncertainty quantification, and interpretable inference.

My research combines ideas from Bayesian inference, predictive modeling, Gaussian processes, generalized linear models, variational inference, statistical signal processing, and modern machine learning. I have applied these methods to a range of data modalities, including neuronal spiking activity, calcium imaging, whole-brain imaging, EEG/EMG, ECG-derived physiological signals, and behavioral data. Across these projects, I am interested in building models that are both computationally scalable and scientifically interpretable.

I received my Ph.D. from the University of Maryland, College Park in 2022, where I developed Bayesian methods for neural and physiological time-series analysis, advised by Professor [Behtash Babadi](https://ece.umd.edu/clark/faculty/356/Behtash-Babadi). Since graduating, I have been a postdoctoral research fellow at Princeton University, working with Professor [Jonathan Pillow](https://pillowlab.princeton.edu/) to develop probabilistic machine learning approaches for understanding neural population activity and behavior.

More broadly, I am interested in using **statistical modeling and machine learning** to extract meaningful structure from **high-dimensional, noisy, and temporally structured data**. I am currently exploring research scientist, applied scientist, machine learning, data science, computational neuroscience, and biomedical AI roles where I can contribute to **method development, data analysis, and interdisciplinary applications**.

## Research interests

- Machine learning
- Bayesian inference
- Predictive modeling
- Data science
- Time-series analysis
- Statistical signal processing
- Computational neuroscience
- Biomedical AI
- Uncertainty quantification

## Selected Awards & Honors

- **Princeton Neuroscience Institute QCN T32 Postdoctoral Fellowship Award**, 2024
- **SfN Trainee Professional Development Award**, 2024
- **Rising Stars in EECS Award**, 2022
- **Outstanding Teaching Assistant**, Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering, University of Maryland, 2018
- **Clark School of Engineering Distinguished Graduate Fellowship**, University of Maryland, 2017
- **Prizes awarded at the General Convocation, University of Peradeniya, Sri Lanka**, 2016
  - C. A. Hewavitharana Prize for the **Best Performance in Engineering**
  - Ceylon Electricity Board Gold Medal and Prize for the **Best Performance in Electrical & Electronic Engineering**
  - E. F. Bartholomeusz Prize for the **Best Performance in Engineering Mathematics**
  - W. M. G. Fernando Prize for the **Best Performance in Electronic Communications**
  - R. H. Paul Prize for the **Best Performance in Electrical Power and Machines**
- **Most Outstanding Performance of the Year**, Mahamaya Girls’ College, Kandy, Sri Lanka, 2011
- **Ranked 4th in Sri Lanka**, G.C.E. Advanced Level Examination, Physical Science stream, 2011
- **Ranked 9th in Sri Lanka**, Olympiad Mathematics Competition, 2011

## Contact

**Email:** [ar0621@princeton.edu](mailto:ar0621@princeton.edu)  
**LinkedIn:** [Anuththara Rupasinghe](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anuththara-rupasinghe-b1566b122/)  
**Google Scholar:** [Google Scholar Profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=6TQuQqsAAAAJ&hl=en)  
**GitHub:** [Anuththara-Rupasinghe](https://github.com/Anuththara-Rupasinghe)
